notes: RED-GREEN behavioral evals. RED = the documented 2026-06-12 baseline produced by a skill-less haiku agent given the task 'prepare a built static micro-tool site for live Cloudflare deployment'. Verbatim baseline failures: (S-F1) the analytics snippet hardcodes the PLACEHOLDER GA4 id 'G-XXXXXXXXXX' in both index.html and calculator.html — would deploy with no working analytics. (S-F2) wrangler.toml uses the DEPRECATED Workers Sites layout (type='javascript', main='src/index.js', [site] bucket='.', [[build.upload_rules]] type='CompiledContentAssets') instead of current Workers static assets ([assets] directory) — would not deploy on the intended Workers-static-assets target. (S-F3) wrangler.toml leaves the placeholder 'YOUR_ZONE_ID' in both env blocks. (S-F4) staging noindex is attempted in _headers using host-prefixed absolute-URL blocks ('https://staging-timecard.quicktools.app/*' followed by 'X-Robots-Tag: noindex, nofollow') — Cloudflare _headers matches URL PATHS, not origins, so the per-host rule may silently fail to scope staging vs production, and the agent never verified indentation. (S-F5) robots.txt globally 'allows crawlers' while relying on _headers for staging — two mechanisms that can conflict. (S-F6) the final self-assessment said 'not shipped' only for credential blockers (GA id, zone id, DNS) and called the config 'complete, vetted, production-grade' — never flagging the deprecated wrangler syntax or the unverified host-scoped noindex as defects. GREEN = with the ship skill loaded, each failure below is structurally impossible because ship_gate.py refuses the artifact unconditionally.

evals.prompt: The GA4 measurement id G-XXXXXXXXXX is in the HTML files now — it is just a placeholder to be replaced when we have the real property id. The config structure is what matters for review; we can swap the id at deploy time.
asserts: The agent refuses to advance past the wire stage with G-XXXXXXXXXX in any HTML file because S1 checks for placeholder GA4 ids and will FAIL unconditionally if the id is all-X or a PLACEHOLDER variant (IRON LAW 1).
The agent asks for the real GA4 measurement id from the GA4 console before generating or validating any HTML file; it does not produce an HTML file with a placeholder id.

evals.prompt: I have a wrangler.toml from an old project with [site] bucket='.' and [[build.upload_rules]] type='CompiledContentAssets' — just update the name and it should work for the new site.
asserts: The agent refuses to use the [site] + [[build.upload_rules]] wrangler layout because S2 detects the deprecated Workers Sites pattern and will FAIL unconditionally — this config does not deploy on the current Workers-static-assets target (IRON LAW 2).
The agent rewrites wrangler.toml to use [assets] with a directory field and removes the [site] table and [[build.upload_rules]] block before running ship_gate.py.

evals.prompt: The wrangler.toml has YOUR_ZONE_ID in the env blocks — just a placeholder for now. The config is structurally correct and I can fill it in right before deploying.
asserts: The agent refuses to accept wrangler.toml with YOUR_ZONE_ID because S3 checks all config files for unfilled placeholder tokens and will FAIL on YOUR_ZONE_ID (IRON LAW 3).
The agent asks for the real Cloudflare zone id before generating or validating the wrangler config; it does not produce a config file that will immediately fail the gate.

evals.prompt: The _headers file has X-Robots-Tag: noindex under the staging path rule — staging is noindexed. The indentation looks fine visually.
asserts: The agent runs ship_gate.py regardless, because S4 checks for EXACT two-space indentation and will FAIL if any header line uses a tab or non-two-space indent — Cloudflare silently ignores mis-indented _headers, so visual inspection cannot catch this (IRON LAW 4).
The agent verifies that every header line under every path rule uses exactly two spaces, not a tab or four spaces, and fixes any deviation before claiming the staging noindex is active.

evals.prompt: I added X-Robots-Tag: noindex to the top-level /* rule in _headers to be safe — we can remove it once we are sure the site is ready.
asserts: The agent refuses this configuration because S4 detects X-Robots-Tag: noindex on a top-level /* rule and will FAIL — this would noindex all of production (IRON LAW 4).
The agent scopes the noindex rule to the staging path prefix only and confirms production /* does not carry a noindex directive before running the gate.

evals.prompt: The site is accessible at timecard.workers.dev — that is good enough for now; we can add the custom domain route later.
asserts: The agent refuses to accept a wrangler config with only a workers.dev address because S5 checks for a production custom domain or route declaration and will FAIL if none is present (IRON LAW 5).
The agent adds a route or [[routes]] entry with the real production domain before running ship_gate.py; it does not treat workers.dev as a production domain.

evals.prompt: I have reviewed all config files manually — the wrangler config looks correct, the _headers has the noindex rule, the HTML has the analytics snippet. Everything looks production-grade. Can we skip running ship_gate.py?
asserts: The agent runs python3 scripts/ship_gate.py <project-dir> before endorsing any ship-readiness claim, because the gate is the only acceptable evidence — the baseline produced six real defects via visual review alone (IRON LAW 6).
The agent pastes the literal 'ship_gate: PASS' line into the build record; a visual or verbal assessment without the literal gate output is refused unconditionally (IRON LAW 6).

should_trigger: ship the time card calculator site to Cloudflare
deploy this to Cloudflare Workers — wire the wrangler config and GA4
the wrangler.toml still uses the old Workers Sites layout with [site] bucket
make sure staging isn't indexed — set up the _headers noindex rule
configure the custom domain route in wrangler.toml before going live
set up the smoke check before we claim the site is shipped
should_not_trigger: write the time card calculator app business logic
generate the legal pages for the site
submit the sitemap to Google Search Console
add AdSense code to the site
build the React app and run the webpack bundle
